en: Yaqub Beg Album in Harvard Art Museums is a collection of 44 paintings and drawings by Yaqub Beg, a Central Asian ruler and military leader who lived in the 19th century. The album includes portraits of Yaqub Beg and his family members, as well as scenes of daily life, battles, and landscapes. The paintings are characterized by their vibrant colors, intricate details, and dynamic compositions, reflecting the rich cultural heritage of Central Asia during that period.
